Rangelands are often the first areas to be impacted by drought due to their sensitivity to moisture levels. Having a rangeland mask in the Hazards watch enables proactive measures to mitigate potential impacts on livestock, wildlife, and ecosystems once early warning signs of drought stress on vegetation are detected.
Highlights areas with significantly above or below normal rainfall, helping stakeholders anticipate droughts or excessive wet conditions

The Heat Stress Index is categorized into three levels: Caution (Green & Blue, 28–34°C) indicates low heat stress, with possible fatigue if exposed for long durations. Extreme Caution (Yellow & Brown, 34–44°C) signals moderate to high heat stress, where the risk of heat exhaustion increases. Danger (Red, 44°C and above) represents severe heat stress, with a high likelihood of heat stroke or collapse, requiring immediate protection.
